programmes.show_description: Courtney Love in conversation with Jo Whiley, Lauren Laverne & Simon Mayo from the archive.

programmes.show_name: The First Time with Courtney Love
programmes.show_description: Courtney Love shares her musical milestones with Matt Everitt in a programme first broadcast in 2014.
As part of the Courtney Love Artist Collection another chance to hear a documentary reflecting the post punk era in Liverpool key to her formative years in the early 1980's.
Karla Chubb of Sprints presents a Courtney Love playlist featuring both material by Hole and solo efforts and reflecting her enduring influence.
The biggest tunes to start the day, your chance to vote on a 'Lost Love', plus will your track get a spin on the wheel?
Wonderful music to start your day, including the Cloudbusting mix to banish the grey skies.
James Blake joins ahead of the release of new album, Trying Times.
Ahead of their sold-out show at Ally Pally, Viagra Boys and Getdown Services put their music tastes to the test in a 6 Music Fight Club! Who wins? You decide!
Professor Sophie Daniels delves deep into Gorillaz's creative family tree in this week's 6 Musicology, and Aurora takes part in this weeks 'Song I Wish I'd Written'.
Scottish American duo Witch Post join Nathan and Deb live in session from Salford, ahead of the release of their second EP Butterfly.
Gideon Coe plays his music loves from every era and genre.
Kelly Lee Owens picks some of her favourite music of Welsh Folk and Tales.
Celebrating Arctic Monkeys' Whatever People Say I Am... with Richard Hawley and Lava La Rue!
Three hours of musical curiosities from Mark Lamarr's record box including past sessions from Graham Coxon and Elvis Costello.
Hope of the States play live in Bristol in 2003, and there's BBC sessions from Charlotte Adigery and Orquesta La Clave.

Names in Songs returns. Can you hear your name in a well-known song?

Actor Cillian Murphy joins around new film, Peaky Blinders: The Immortal Man.
Craig celebrates your midweek wins for Wednesday, I'm In Love, and we hear another of your left-field music suggestions as we turn Left At The Lights.
